Name: Driving Gale-Caller
Summary: High-value caster that can repeatedly displace the group.
Description:
[Kick] Interrupt every Repel cast.
[Positioning] Save stops for this mob if multiple casters are in the pull.
[Priority] This is one of the cleanest first-kill targets in Skyreach trash.

Name: Adorned Bladetalon
Summary: Dangerous bird add with bleed pressure on both tank and random players.
Description:
[Bleed] Blade Rush hits two random players plus the tank, so be ready with bleed cleanses, personals, and fast healing.
[Tank] Shear can overlap with Blade Rush, making back-to-back physical damage especially dangerous.
[Priority] Treat these mobs as major defensive checks in larger pulls.

Name: Blinding Sun Priestess
Summary: Solar caster that combines a mandatory interrupt with an enemy shield buff.
Description:
[Kick] Stop every Blinding Light cast.
[Purge] Remove Solar Barrier immediately or swap hard onto the shielded mob.
[Priority] Keep this mob under control before committing damage elsewhere in the pack.

Name: Solar Elemental
Summary: Construct trash that spawns a must-kill orb while layering floor effects.
Description:
[Priority Target] Swap to the Solar Orb the moment it spawns and kill it before it snowballs.
[Dodge] Keep moving out of Solar Fire circles.
[Pull Control] Avoid overpulling around this mob because the orb demands immediate target swaps.

Name: Sun Talon Tamer
Summary: Handler mob that marks players for lethal chase pressure.
Description:
[Kite] If you are marked by Mark of Death, create distance early and use a personal if other damage is incoming.
[Awareness] Make sure the party is not stacked behind the marked player.
[Priority] This mob becomes much scarier when paired with other random-target damage.

Name: Raging Squall
Summary: Enrage-heavy storm mob with extra melee damage and a death zone.
Description:
[Tank] Wind Claws adds extra nature damage to its swings, so respect melee uptime.
[Soothe] Remove Wrathful Wind when possible.
[Dodge] Step out of the Wind Blast area the mob leaves behind on death.
